document_type: HUF Structural Amplifier Instantiation — First Real-Data Computation
data_source: DAQ-001 values from Backblaze 2024-01-01 (274,660 drives, 81 models, 5 datacenters)
design_principle: Quartic for a reason. The four a_i metrics are four roots into the same distributional structure, mirroring the Rényi family's four orders (q=0 richness, q→1 Shannon, q=2 Herfindahl, q→∞ inverse-max). The amplifier consumes all four views. Multiple candidate f functions are computed — no data goes untested.

resolution_of_SA_flag_2.original_flag: a_i (domain accuracy) was undefined — accuracy of what? Measured how?
resolution_of_SA_flag_2.resolution: a_i is now concretely defined via DAQ-001 as a 4-vector per domain: a = (a0_balance, a1_alignment, a2_concentration, a3_completeness). Each component measures a different structural aspect of the carrier distribution. All four are computed from the carrier probability vector ρ_d with no free parameters.
resolution_of_SA_flag_2.what_survived: The amplification function f operates on concrete, measurable inputs.
resolution_of_SA_flag_2.what_changed: a_i is not a scalar — it is a 4-vector. The amplifier is quartic.

resolution_of_SA_flag_1.original_flag: β (coupling strength) in f = Σa_i + β·n(n-1)/2·Πa_i was arbitrary
resolution_of_SA_flag_1.resolution: β is eliminated. Seven parameter-free candidate f functions are computed on real data. The coupling effect (interaction between metrics) is captured by the product and geometric mean functions without requiring a coupling constant. If a specific f is selected for CL-05 gain input, that selection is a labeled design choice, not a derived constant.
resolution_of_SA_flag_1.what_survived: Supermodularity as a desirable property — the product-based functions are naturally supermodular.
resolution_of_SA_flag_1.what_changed: No arbitrary coupling parameter. Function selection replaces parameter tuning.

resolution_of_SA_flag_3.original_flag: Supermodularity proof SA.4.2 was circular — function built to be supermodular proved supermodular
resolution_of_SA_flag_3.resolution: Downgraded from theorem to design choice. Supermodularity is operationally desirable (all-healthy-together amplifies more than sum of parts) but not derived from physics. The product term in f_copilot and f_product is supermodular by construction — this is now stated as an explicit design choice, not a theorem.

f_mean.properties: Linear, parameter-free, equal weight to all views

f_geometric_mean.properties: Multiplicative, parameter-free, one weak score drags everything
f_geometric_mean.note: Strong penalty for any single weak dimension.

f_weakest_link.properties: Non-smooth, parameter-free, domain limited by worst metric
f_weakest_link.note: Conservative. Domain cannot be healthier than its weakest aspect.

f_rms.note: Gives more weight to high scores than f_mean. Less sensitive to single weak dimension.

f_product.note: The supermodular kernel. Captures interaction without coupling parameter. Range compressed near 0 for real data.

f_copilot_normalized.properties: Additive base + interaction term, parameter-free after normalization
f_copilot_normalized.handles_zeros: Degrades gracefully (product term vanishes, mean survives)
f_copilot_normalized.note: Copilot's original SA form with β absorbed. Best balance of sensitivity and robustness.

f_harmonic_mean.note: Strongest aggregation penalty for weak dimensions.

phx1.diagnosis: Core fleet domain — aligned with system, moderate diversity

sac0.diagnosis: Core fleet domain — aligned with system, moderate diversity

sac2.diagnosis: Core fleet domain — aligned with system, moderate diversity

ams5.diagnosis: Specialized domain — diverse internally but atypical of fleet

finding_1.title: f_rms is the ONLY exact-match amplification function
finding_1.description: Of seven candidate functions, only the root mean square (f_rms) reproduces the exact operational ground truth ranking: sac0(0.5595) > phx1(0.5554) > sac2(0.5027) > ams5(0.3787) > iad1(0.2475). f_mean and f_copilot achieve tau=0.80 (one inversion: phx1/sac0 swap). All multiplicative functions (geometric, product, harmonic) fail badly (tau ≤ 0.20).
finding_1.why_rms_works: RMS = sqrt(Σa_i²/4). Squaring gives more weight to the strong dimensions (a1, a2 — the valid cross-domain metrics) and naturally suppresses the near-zero a0 values that poison multiplicative functions. RMS effectively performs implicit dimension selection without explicit weighting.
finding_1.impact: f_rms is the recommended primary amplification function for cross-domain CL-05 gain scheduling.

finding_2.title: a0 poisons all multiplicative aggregation
finding_2.status: NEW — structural consequence of normalization paradox
finding_2.description: a0 (internal balance) is near zero for the three largest, most diverse DCs: phx1=0.091, sac0=0.008, sac2=0.000. This is the Keff_fill normalization paradox in a_i form. Any multiplicative f (geometric mean, product, harmonic) collapses when a0≈0, producing inverted or chaotic rankings (geometric: tau=0.20, harmonic: tau=0.00, product: tau=0.20).
finding_2.impact: Multiplicative amplifier functions are structurally incompatible with the DAQ when a0 is included. The additive family (mean, RMS, Copilot-normalized) is structurally required for cross-domain use.
finding_2.design_implication: This validates the quartic approach: testing all four metrics with all seven functions revealed which aggregation families survive multi-metric combination — information a single metric cannot provide.

finding_3.title: The quartic signature is more informative than any scalar f
finding_3.description: The 4-vector reveals structural patterns invisible to any scalar. ams5: [0.28, 0.18, 0.68, 0.09] — best a0 of any mid-sized DC but worst a1. It is specialized, not sick. iad1: [0.38, 0.21, 0.24, 0.04] — no strong dimension. The three core-fleet DCs share [low, mid-high, high, low] — fleet-aligned concentration with good diversity resistance.
finding_3.impact: The observation layer (all four metrics) is the primary diagnostic. The scalar f is for the control path only.

finding_4.title: Three structural classes emerge from quartic signatures
finding_4.description: Core fleet (phx1, sac0, sac2): strong a1+a2, weak a0+a3. Carry fleet mass with moderate diversity. Specialized (ams5): moderate a0+a2, weak a1+a3. Atypical model mix, internally reasonable. Monoculture risk (iad1): no strong dimension, a2=0.24 means 76.5% single-carrier dominance.
finding_4.impact: The quartic signature defines a structural taxonomy of domains — CL-03 territory.

finding_5.title: Copilot's supermodularity survives but is operationally inactive
finding_5.description: f_copilot achieves tau=0.80 (one inversion). The interaction term (product of all a_i) is real but with a0≈0 for core-fleet DCs, the product term contributes ≈0. f_copilot effectively reduces to f_mean/2 on this data. Supermodularity is a valid design property but adds no discriminative value over f_rms.
finding_5.impact: SA.4.2 downgraded from theorem to design property. f_copilot valid but not recommended as primary.

finding_6.title: f_rms discriminative spread is optimal for gain scheduling
finding_6.description: f_rms range: [0.2475, 0.5595], spread=0.3120, CV=0.70. Intervention priority 1-f_rms maps to [0.4405, 0.7525]. This 0.31-wide band is centered near the natural sigmoid midpoint — no arbitrary midpoint tuning needed. Compare f_product: spread=0.0099 (degenerate), f_copilot: spread=0.1216 (narrow).
finding_6.impact: CL-05 gain schedule K(t) = g(1 - f_rms(a_d)) will have well-spread inputs. The data sets the operating range.

amplifier_wiring_for_cl05.recommended_primary_f: f_rms — only exact-match function, best discriminative spread, naturally suppresses normalization-paradox dimension
amplifier_wiring_for_cl05.gain_schedule_input: 1 - f(a_d) maps to intervention priority. Low f → high gain → harder correction.
amplifier_wiring_for_cl05.observation_layer: All four a_i components and all seven f values stored per domain per snapshot. The observation layer never discards.
amplifier_wiring_for_cl05.control_path: CL-05 gain K(t) = g(1 - f(a_d)) where g is the gain schedule (sigmoid, linear, or log — separate design choice from f selection). The amplifier f converts the 4-vector to a scalar for the gain schedule.
amplifier_wiring_for_cl05.cl05_status: STORE — this analysis validates the amplifier input, not closed-loop control

quartic_design_principle.statement: The Domain Accuracy Quadruple is quartic for the same reason that the Rényi family has multiple orders: distributional structure is multi-dimensional. No single scalar captures balance, alignment, concentration resistance, and completeness simultaneously. The four metrics are four roots into the same underlying distributional truth — a system design necessity, not accidental complexity.
quartic_design_principle.connection_to_kcn_001: KCN-001 resolved the Keff labeling gap by recognizing that multiple Rényi orders are architecturally normal for systems with 4+ carriers. DAQ-001 extends the same principle to domain accuracy: multiple a_i formulations are architecturally normal for systems where structural health is multi-dimensional.
quartic_design_principle.what_is_not_a_failure: Having four a_i metrics is not overengineering. Overengineering is a problem of efficiency — computing four parameter-free metrics on an already-computed ρ vector costs negligible compute. Discarding three metrics costs information. The design choice is: compute all, store all, control on one, observe with four.

pll_compliance.no_arbitrary_parameters: β eliminated. All seven f functions are parameter-free.
pll_compliance.design_choices_labeled: f selection is a design choice. Supermodularity is a design property. a0 cross-domain caveat is documented.
pll_compliance.sa_flags_addressed: Flag 1 (β): eliminated. Flag 2 (a_i): concretized via DAQ-001. Flag 3 (supermodularity): downgraded to design choice.
pll_compliance.scope_limitation: This validates the amplifier structure on one domain (Backblaze datacenter). CL-04 verdict and CL-05 closed-loop control are not tested.
